title: English Deep Analysis Schema
description: Schema for deep per-document analysis of English court judgments. Extracts analytical dimensions not captured by base schema or structural segmentation: complexity, reasoning quality, legal doctrine, and outcome-relevant features.

case_complexity.description: Overall complexity assessment of the case across multiple dimensions.

overall_score.description: Complexity score from 1 (simple, single-issue) to 5 (highly complex, multi-party, novel law). Base this on number of parties, issues, length of reasoning, novelty of legal questions.

factual_complexity.description: How factually complex is the case? 'simple' = single event, few parties; 'moderate' = multiple events or parties; 'complex' = extended timeline, many parties, conflicting evidence.

legal_complexity.description: How legally complex? 'simple' = straightforward application of settled law; 'moderate' = some interpretive questions; 'complex' = novel points, conflicting authorities, multiple legal frameworks.

num_legal_issues.description: Count of distinct legal issues or grounds addressed by the court.

num_statutes_cited.description: Count of distinct statutes or Acts of Parliament cited.

num_cases_cited.description: Count of distinct case law authorities cited.

legal_domains.description: Primary legal domains or offence categories involved in this case. Select all that apply.

reasoning_patterns.description: Types of legal reasoning employed by the court. Select all that apply.

x-enum-labels.analogical_reasoning: Reasoning by analogy to similar cases
x-enum-labels.factual_inference: Drawing factual inferences from circumstantial evidence

reasoning_quality_score.description: Quality of the court's reasoning from 1 to 5. 1 = cursory, conclusory, little analysis. 3 = adequate, addresses main points. 5 = thorough, well-structured, considers all angles, clear chain of logic. Base this on: depth of analysis, engagement with arguments, clarity of reasoning chain.

reasoning_quality_explanation.description: Brief explanation (2-3 sentences) of why you assigned the reasoning quality score. What makes the reasoning strong or weak?

principle.description: Statement of the legal principle articulated or applied

source.description: Source of the principle: statute name, case citation, or 'common law'

how_applied.description: How the court applied this principle to the facts (1-2 sentences)

key_legal_principles.description: Key legal principles articulated, applied, or developed in this judgment. Extract the most important 3-5 principles.

significance.description: Why this precedent matters in this case (1 sentence)

precedent_treatment.description: How the court treated cited precedents. Extract the 3-5 most significant case citations. Null if no precedents discussed.

statutory_interpretation_methods.description: Methods of statutory interpretation used by the court. Null if no statutory interpretation involved.

burden_and_standard_of_proof.description: Discussion of burden and standard of proof if addressed. Null if not discussed.

standard_discussed.description: What standard of proof was discussed

burden_issues.description: Any specific burden of proof issues raised (reverse burden, evidential burden, etc.)

procedural_fairness_issues.description: Any procedural fairness concerns raised or discussed: jury directions, disclosure, abuse of process, delay, bad character evidence handling. Null if none.

judicial_tone.description: Predominant tone of the judgment. 'neutral_analytical' = dispassionate legal analysis; 'critical' = critical of lower court or parties; 'sympathetic' = acknowledges hardship; 'deferential' = defers to lower court/jury; 'emphatic' = strong conviction in reasoning; 'cautious' = hedged, careful qualifications.

policy_considerations.description: Policy considerations mentioned by the court: public protection, deterrence, rehabilitation, public interest, victim impact, proportionality, consistency. Null if no policy discussion.

factor.description: The factor that was most decisive

weight.description: How much weight the court gave this factor

outcome_determinative_factors.description: The 3-5 factors that most determined the outcome. What tipped the balance? These are the factors a lawyer would focus on when predicting outcomes in similar cases.

novel_legal_points.description: Any novel or unusual legal points raised or decided. Points where the court developed the law, addressed a question of first impression, or resolved a circuit split. Null if no novel points.

practice_implications.description: Practical implications for legal practitioners. What should lawyers take away from this judgment? E.g. 'Defence solicitors should ensure X', 'Judges should direct juries on Y', 'The CPS should consider Z when charging'. Null if no clear practice implications.

cross_reference_potential.description: How useful is this judgment for cross-referencing with other cases.

precedential_value.description: 'high' = establishes or clarifies a legal principle; 'medium' = useful application of existing law; 'low' = routine application; 'none' = highly fact-specific with no broader applicability.

research_value.description: 'high' = comprehensive review of law in this area; 'medium' = useful discussion; 'low' = minimal analysis.

suggested_search_queries.description: 3-5 semantic search queries that would help find similar or related judgments. Write these as natural language queries a legal researcher would use.

document_quality.description: Assessment of the document text quality (OCR issues, completeness).

text_quality.description: 'clean' = no OCR or formatting issues; 'minor_issues' = occasional typos or formatting glitches; 'significant_issues' = noticeable OCR errors affecting readability; 'poor' = substantial text corruption.

completeness.description: Whether the document appears to contain the full judgment text.

quality_notes.description: Brief notes on any specific quality issues observed (e.g. 'paragraphs 15-20 have OCR artifacts', 'document appears truncated mid-sentence').

analysis_confidence.description: Your overall confidence in this analysis. 'high' = clear, well-structured judgment with confident analysis. 'medium' = some ambiguity but reasonable analysis. 'low' = poor document quality, unusual format, or highly ambiguous content.
